chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT AT LARKANA 1st. Cr. Bail Appln. No. S-128 of 2026 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For order on M.A No. 1198/2026. (Urgency Application) 2. For order on office objection. 3. For order on M.A No. 1199/2026. (Exemption Application) 4. For hearing of bail application. 12-03-2026 Ms. Bakhtawar Abro, advocate for the applicant a/w the applicant.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency is granted. 2. to 4. Through the instant criminal bail application, the applicant Dildar @ Teendo seeks protective bail in Crime No. 63/2025, registered at Police Station Dokri, for the offences punishable under Sections 402, 399, 324, 353, 148 and 149, P.P.C. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that the applicant has been falsely implicated in this case due to political rivalry. She further submits that even otherwise the allegations against the applicant are general in nature, and though straight firing is alleged, no injury has been caused to anyone. She further contends that the police are bent upon arresting the applicant in order to disgrace and humiliate him; therefore, she seeks protective bail so that the applicant may surrender before the learned trial Court and seek appropriate remedy in accordance with law. Without touching the merits of the case, the applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- (Rupees Thirty Thousand only) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. In the meantime, the Non- Bailable Warrants, if any, issued against the applicant shall remain suspended.

Without touching the merits of the case, the applicant is admitted to protective bail for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- (Rupees Thirty Thousand only) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. In the meantime, the Non- Bailable Warrants, if any, issued against the applicant shall remain suspended. This order shall cease to have effect after the expiry of five days from today or as soon as the applicant surrenders before the concerned Court, whichever occurs earlier. The bail application is disposed of accordingly. Judge Abdul Salam/P.A
